ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for beef pepperoni pizza with hot paprika honey; closest_parent 'pizza food product' used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers pizza as a category; no specific code for beef pepperoni variant with hot paprika honey topping.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226754000 is the generic pizza concept; no specific code for beef pepperoni pizza variant.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole personal-sized beef pepperoni pizza weighing approximately 300 g, as typically served at a Singapore pizza restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza with meat topping, baked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21299) and USDA Foundation Foods beef pepperoni component
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pizza with meat topping (NDB 21299) and cross-referenced with USDA beef pepperoni data. Values adjusted upward slightly for sodium and fat to reflect the hot paprika honey variant with additional chilli flakes. This is a restaurant-prepared item sold in Singapore; no direct HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for this specific product. USDA SR Legacy pizza with meat topping is the closest approved source match.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pizza and mixed grain bread products — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008). International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 50 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) international GI tables, which report pizza (cheese and tomato) GI values in the range 30–60 with a representative value of approximately 50 for standard pizza. Beef pepperoni pizza with tomato base is nutritionally analogous; the addition of hot paprika honey and chilli flakes does not materially alter the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 x 93.6) / 100 = 46.8, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 31.2 x 3 = 93.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (680 mg per 100 g; approximately 2040 mg per full pizza serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this pizza due to its very high sodium content, which can significantly contribute to exceeding the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; a single serving may approach or exceed this threshold.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 50) with high glycemic load per serving (GL ~47) due to large carbohydrate portion from pizza dough
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this pizza in small portions and infrequently, as the glycemic load per full serving is high despite a moderate GI; pairing with a salad or non-starchy vegetables may help blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (3.9 g per 100 g), high sodium (680 mg per 100 g), and processed beef pepperoni as a red and processed meat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this pizza due to its combination of high sodium, saturated fat from beef pepperoni and cheese, and the presence of processed red meat, all of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hot Beef Pepperoni Pizza is a baked pizza featuring a tomato sauce base topped with sliced beef pepperoni, hot paprika, honey, and chilli flakes, served at Now Pizza in Funan Mall, Singapore. The crust is a standard wheat-flour pizza base, baked until golden and crisp at the edges. The flavour profile is savoury and spicy with a subtle sweetness from the honey and heat from both the paprika and chilli flakes. The beef pepperoni provides a smoky, salty, and slightly fatty character typical of cured meat toppings. Per 100 g, this pizza provides approximately 266 kcal, 11.8 g protein, 10.4 g fat (of which 3.9 g is saturated), and 31.2 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is high at 680 mg per 100 g, making a full serving a significant source of dietary sodium. The glycemic index is estimated at 50 (low-moderate), but the glycemic load per full pizza serving is high due to the large carbohydrate contribution from the dough. Clinically, this pizza is a concern for individuals managing hypertension (very high sodium per serving), cardiovascular disease (processed red meat, saturated fat, sodium), and Type 2 Diabetes (high glycemic load per serving). It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten or dairy. The dish is sold at SGD 16.40 per pizza at Now Pizza, Funan, Singapore.
common_user_queries: What is hot beef pepperoni pizza?

confidence_reason: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or equivalent primary regional source entry exists for this specific restaurant product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pizza with meat topping (NDB 21299), which is the closest approved source. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous pizza entries. Confidence is medium rather than low because the USDA SR Legacy source is a well-validated approved secondary source and the food type (pizza with meat topping) is well-characterised.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza with meat topping source entry. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry. The food name in the input record was 'Hot Bee Beef Pepperoni', which appears to be a truncated or abbreviated menu name; the canonical English name has been normalised to 'Hot Beef Pepperoni Pizza' for clarity and retrieval. The halal status is listed as false because beef pepperoni sourced from non-halal-certified suppliers is common; the restaurant's halal certification status was not confirmed from an approved source. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI 50 x 93.6 g carbohydrate per 300 g serving) / 100 = 46.8.
